title: 谋杀启事
summary:
Inspector Corrado travels by Daimler to an ancient mansion, which served as the former home of Mrs. Goddard. Upon arrival, he is greeted by a butler and meets Nurse MacLane. The nurse warns Corrado that Mrs. Goddard is a dying woman, kept alive primarily by morphine and a strong will to survive, and that she may suddenly collapse into a semi-comatose state during their meeting.

Corrado is led to a large bedroom to interview the frail but spirited Bella Goddard. During the conversation, Mrs. Goddard discusses her relationship with her late husband, Randall, and her connection to Blanche Blacklock. She explains that Randall left his money to Blanche because they had no surviving children—their only son died at age two—and because he trusted Blanche’s upright character and judgment. Mrs. Goddard notes that Blanche had previously helped Randall with financial difficulties. She also describes the difficult upbringing of the Blacklock sisters, noting that Letitia is a responsible woman who returned home to care for her disabled sister after their tyrannical father died.

When questioned about other potential heirs, Mrs. Goddard reveals that Randall had a sister, Sonya, but they were estranged because Randall disapproved of her marriage to Dimetri Stanfordtis. Mrs. Goddard believes Dimetri was a genuine fraud with a criminal record, though she notes that Sonya was aware of this and loved him regardless. Despite her chronic illness and losses, Mrs. Goddard expresses a sense of gratitude for the life she has lived.
